ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) ProxyLogon/ProxyShell post-exploitation: w3wp.exe spawning recon/credential-access children on Exchange
ucs.description: Detects Silk Typhoon's signature on-prem Exchange exploitation chain — after dropping a China Chopper-style webshell via ProxyLogon (CVE-2021-26855) or ProxyShell (CVE-2021-34473/-31207/-34523), the IIS worker process w3wp.exe (running under MSExchange* app pools) spawns cmd/powershell to run domain recon (nltest/net), enumerate mailboxes, drop ASPX webshells, and stage ProcDump/comsvcs.dll against LSASS for credential theft.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_name) as child_proc from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" (Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","hostname.exe","systeminfo.exe","nltest.exe","ipconfig.exe","ping.exe","tasklist.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","rundll32.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","wmic.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe","makecab.exe")) by host user Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval suspicious=case(match(process,"(?i)comsvcs\.dll.*MiniDump"),"lsass_dump", match(process,"(?i)lsass"),"lsass_dump", match(process,"(?i)nltest.*/dclist|/domain_trusts"),"ad_recon", match(process,"(?i)New-MailboxExportRequest|Get-Mailbox"),"mailbox_collection", match(process,"(?i)\.aspx|aspnet_client"),"webshell_drop", 1=1,"lolbin_chain") | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) — Exchange w3wp.exe → recon/credtheft chain (ProxyLogon/ProxyShell)
let _exchange_lolbin_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","hostname.exe","systeminfo.exe","nltest.exe","ipconfig.exe","ping.exe","tasklist.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","rundll32.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","wmic.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe","makecab.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
let _proc_chain = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"                       // IIS worker
    | where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("MSExchange","OWA","ECP","FrontEnd")  // Exchange app-pools
    | where FileName in~ (_exchange_lolbin_children)
    | extend Suspicion = case(
        ProcessCommandLine has_any ("comsvcs.dll","MiniDump","lsass"), "lsass_dump",
        ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/dclist","/domain_trusts","net group","Domain Admins","Enterprise Admins"), "ad_recon",
        ProcessCommandLine has_any ("New-MailboxExportRequest","Get-Mailbox","Search-Mailbox","New-ManagementRoleAssignment"), "mailbox_collection",
        ProcessCommandLine has_any ("aspnet_client",".aspx","OutlookEN.aspx","OAB","Echo.aspx"), "webshell_drop",
        ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-EncodedCommand","FromBase64String","DownloadString","IEX"), "obf_powershell",
        "lolbin_chain")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              ChildBin = FileName, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              Suspicion, SHA256, ReportId, DeviceId;
// Pivot — pair with ASPX/DLL drops by w3wp on the same host within ±10m (China Chopper artifact)
let _file_drops = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed")
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\", @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\", @"\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\", @"\inetpub\wwwroot\")
    | where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".dll"
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DroppedFile = FileName, DroppedPath = FolderPath, DropSHA256 = SHA256;
_proc_chain
| join kind=leftouter _file_drops on DeviceId
| where isnull(DropTime) or DropTime between (Timestamp - 10m .. Timestamp + 10m)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, Suspicion,
          ParentCmd, ChildBin, ChildCmd,
          DroppedFile, DroppedPath, SHA256, DropSHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) — w3wp.exe → recon/credtheft on Exchange (Sentinel SecurityEvent 4688)
let _exchange_lolbin_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","hostname.exe","systeminfo.exe","nltest.exe","ipconfig.exe","ping.exe","tasklist.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","rundll32.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","wmic.exe","7z.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688                                       // process create (requires audit policy)
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
| extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,"\\")[-1]))
| where ChildBin in (_exchange_lolbin_children)
| where Account !endswith "$"                                 // strip machine accts (rare for w3wp children but defensive)
| extend Suspicion = case(
    CommandLine has_any ("comsvcs.dll","MiniDump","lsass"), "lsass_dump",
    CommandLine has_any ("/dclist","/domain_trusts","net group","Domain Admins","Enterprise Admins"), "ad_recon",
    CommandLine has_any ("New-MailboxExportRequest","Get-Mailbox","Search-Mailbox","New-ManagementRoleAssignment"), "mailbox_collection",
    CommandLine has_any ("aspnet_client",".aspx","OAB"), "webshell_drop",
    CommandLine has_any ("-EncodedCommand","FromBase64String","DownloadString","IEX"), "obf_powershell",
    "lolbin_chain")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, ChildBin, CommandLine, Suspicion
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) Exchange Webshell Post-Exploitation
id: 8c0a1f2e-7d11-4a4b-9f2c-silktyphoon-w3wp-children
status: stable
description: |
  IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) running an MSExchange app-pool spawns a recon /
  credential-access / web-shell-drop child. Signature behavior of Silk Typhoon
  (HAFNIUM) post-ProxyLogon and post-ProxyShell exploitation.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2025/03/05/silk-typhoon-targeting-it-supply-chain/
author: detection-engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1003.001
  - g0125
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent_w3wp:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
    ParentCommandLine|contains:
      - 'MSExchange'
      - 'OWA'
      - 'ECP'
      - 'FrontEnd'
  suspicious_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\procdump.exe'
      - '\procdump64.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\7z.exe'
  condition: parent_w3wp and suspicious_child
falsepositives:
  - Exchange cmdlets executed via legitimate scheduled monitoring (rare; allowlist by host+cmdline)
  - Microsoft Exchange CU/SU installations (will burst at known patch windows)
level: high
ucs.rationale: w3wp.exe spawning interactive shells/LOLBins is the canonical Hafnium / Silk Typhoon ProxyLogon and ProxyShell IOC documented by MSTIC, CISA AA21-062A and Mandiant — webshells dropped to \aspnet_client\ and the Exchange OWA auth path are run inside the IIS app-pool, then used to fire net/nltest enumeration and comsvcs.dll/procdump LSASS dumps. Pairing parent=w3wp.exe (Exchange app pool) with child
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon cloud pivot: covert credential addition to existing AAD service principal / app registration
ucs.description: Catches Silk Typhoon's 2024-2025 supply-chain and IT-vendor pivot tradecraft documented by MSTIC — abuse of stolen API keys / OAuth tokens to add new client secrets or certificates (keyCredentials/passwordCredentials) onto pre-existing OAuth applications and service principals belonging to MSPs, RMM tools, or privileged identity-management apps, enabling long-lived tenant-to-tenant access (MITRE T1098.001).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Change.command) as op values(Change.src) as src_ip values(Change.user_agent) as ua from datamodel=Change where (Change.object_category IN ("application","service_principal","app_registration")) (Change.action IN ("updated","created","modified")) (Change.command IN ("Add service principal credentials","Update application – Certificates and secrets management","Update application","Update service principal","Add owner to application","Add owner to service principal","Add app role assignment to service principal")) by Change.user Change.object Change.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Change)` | rename object as app_or_sp | join type=left user [ search index=azure sourcetype="azure:aad:signin" earliest=-30d UserPrincipalName=* | stats values(IPAddress) as known_ips by UserPrincipalName | rename UserPrincipalName as user ] | eval new_ip=if(isnull(known_ips) OR NOT (src_ip IN (split(mvjoin(known_ips,","),","))),1,0) | where new_ip=1 OR match(op,"(?i)credential") | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon — credential added to an existing AAD app/service principal (T1098.001)
// Highest-fidelity when the actor is NOT in their normal admin geo and the targeted app is privileged
let _privileged_app_keywords = dynamic(["PIM","Privileged","RMM","ConnectWise","Kaseya","NinjaOne","Datto","N-able","Intune","Graph","Lighthouse","PartnerCenter","Defender","Sentinel","Exchange","SharePoint","KeyVault"]);
let _cred_ops = dynamic([
    "Add service principal credentials.",
    "Update application - Certificates and secrets management",
    "Update application – Certificates and secrets management",
    "Update application",
    "Update service principal",
    "Add owner to application",
    "Add owner to service principal",
    "Add app role assignment to service principal",
    "Consent to application"
]);
let _events = CloudAppEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where Application in~ ("Microsoft Azure Active Directory","Office 365")
    | where ActionType in~ (_cred_ops)
    | extend Target = tostring(ActivityObjects[0].Name),
             TargetType = tostring(ActivityObjects[0].Type),
             Initiator = AccountDisplayName
    | project Timestamp, ActionType, Application, Initiator, AccountObjectId,
              IPAddress, CountryCode, City, ISP, IsAnonymousProxy, UserAgent,
              Target, TargetType, RawEventData, ReportId;
// Anomaly join — initiator's typical sign-in countries over the last 30d
let _baseline_geo = AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp between (ago(30d) .. ago(1d))
    | where ErrorCode == 0
    | summarize KnownCountries = make_set(Country, 50), KnownIPs = make_set(IPAddress, 200) by AccountObjectId;
_events
| join kind=leftouter _baseline_geo on AccountObjectId
| extend NewGeo = iff(isempty(CountryCode) or KnownCountries has CountryCode, false, true)
| extend PrivilegedTarget = iff(Target has_any (_privileged_app_keywords), true, false)
| where NewGeo or PrivilegedTarget or IsAnonymousProxy or ActionType has "credentials"
| project Timestamp, Initiator, ActionType, Target, TargetType, PrivilegedTarget,
          IPAddress, CountryCode, City, ISP, NewGeo, IsAnonymousProxy, UserAgent
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon — covert credential addition to existing AAD app/service principal
// Sentinel native: AuditLogs (Entra) + SigninLogs anomaly enrichment
let _privileged_app_keywords = dynamic(["PIM","Privileged","RMM","ConnectWise","Kaseya","NinjaOne","Datto","N-able","Intune","Graph","Lighthouse","PartnerCenter","Defender","Sentinel","Exchange","SharePoint","KeyVault"]);
let _cred_ops = dynamic([
    "Add service principal credentials.",
    "Update application - Certificates and secrets management",
    "Update application – Certificates and secrets management",
    "Update application",
    "Update service principal",
    "Add owner to application",
    "Add owner to service principal",
    "Add app role assignment to service principal",
    "Consent to application"
]);
let _changes = AuditLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where OperationName in~ (_cred_ops) or OperationName has_any ("service principal credentials","Certificates and secrets")
    | extend Initiator   = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.userPrincipalName),
             InitiatorIP = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.ipAddress),
             InitiatorObjId = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.id),
             AppName     = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName),
             AppId       = tostring(TargetResources[0].id),
             ModifiedKeys = tostring(TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties)
    | where isnotempty(Initiator) or isnotempty(InitiatorObjId)
    | extend AddedCredential = ModifiedKeys has_any ("KeyCredential","PasswordCredential","keyCredentials","passwordCredentials")
    | project TimeGenerated, OperationName, Result, Initiator, InitiatorObjId, InitiatorIP, AppName, AppId, AddedCredential, ModifiedKeys;
let _baseline = SigninLogs
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(30d) .. ago(1d))
    | where ResultType == 0
    | extend Country = tostring(parse_json(tostring(LocationDetails)).countryOrRegion)
    | summarize KnownCountries = make_set(Country, 50), KnownIPs = make_set(IPAddress, 200) by UserPrincipalName;
_changes
| join kind=leftouter _baseline on $left.Initiator == $right.UserPrincipalName
| join kind=leftouter (
    SigninLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and ResultType == 0
    | extend Country = tostring(parse_json(tostring(LocationDetails)).countryOrRegion)
    | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, IPAddress, Country, UserAgent) by UserPrincipalName
  ) on $left.Initiator == $right.UserPrincipalName
| extend NewGeo = iff(isempty(Country) or (KnownCountries has Country), false, true)
| extend PrivilegedApp = iff(AppName has_any (_privileged_app_keywords), true, false)
| where AddedCredential or NewGeo or PrivilegedApp
| project TimeGenerated, OperationName, Initiator, InitiatorIP, AppName, AppId,
          AddedCredential, PrivilegedApp, RecentCountry = Country, NewGeo,
          RecentSignInIP = IPAddress, RecentUA = UserAgent, ModifiedKeys
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: MSTIC's March 2025 reporting on Silk Typhoon shifted the actor from on-prem zero-day exploitation to abusing stolen API keys and OAuth secrets — adding new keyCredentials/passwordCredentials to legitimate applications (often privileged IT/RMM/PIM apps) gives them durable access that survives password resets and bypasses MFA. The detection joins the credential-addition AuditLog event to the initiat
